headline: Uber Expands Into Air Taxis And Autonomy To Broaden Mobility Platform
summary: Uber Technologies (NYSE:UBER) launched Uber Air in Dubai through a partnership with Joby Aviation, introducing electric air taxi services. The company also introduced Uber Autonomous Solutions, a new platform focused on scalable commercialization of autonomous vehicles. These announcements mark Uber's push beyond traditional ride hailing into urban air mobility and self driving technology. For you as an investor, these moves show how NYSE:UBER is leaning further into its role as a broader...

headline: Joby And Uber Test Commercial Electric Air Taxis In Exclusive Dubai Launch
summary: Joby Aviation (NYSE:JOBY) and Uber plan to launch all electric air taxi services in Dubai this year. Rides are expected to be bookable directly through the Uber app for what is being described as the first commercial deployment of all electric air taxis. Joby has secured an exclusive agreement with Dubai's Road and Transport Authority for this service. For anyone tracking urban air mobility, this move puts Joby Aviation, listed as NYSE:JOBY, on center stage. The company focuses on electric...

headline: Ivo expands to London and New York as revenue grows 6x and Fortune 500 adoption surges
summary: Global expansion follows 500% ARR growth and rapid adoption across enterprise legal teamsSan Francisco, March 05, 2026 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- Contracts sit at the center of every major business relationship, yet most companies still struggle to unlock the insights inside them. Ivo, the market-leading AI-powered contract intelligence platform, has seen demand surge over the past year as in-house legal teams look for faster, more reliable ways to review and extract intelligence from their agreements.

headline: US House committee wants travel companies to answer questions on use of AI for pricing
summary: The chair of the U.S. ‌House Oversight Committee on ‌Thursday asked the CEOs of five ​major travel companies including Uber, Lyft and Expedia to disclose if they were using ‌surveillance pricing ⁠of consumers to hike costs.  Representative James Comer, ⁠the Republican chair of the committee, raised concern in ​letters to ​the ​companies that the ‌rise of surveillance pricing algorithms and use of highly personalized consumer data may create opportunities "for companies to weaponize ‌personal data and ​pad their profit ​margins at ​the expense of ‌providing transparency to consumers."
